jobs.title: Qualified SWPPP Developer (QSD) / Senior Environmental Compliance Specialist
jobs.description:
BKF is a multi\-service infrastructure consulting firm providing civil engineering, construction management, environmental, planning, and surveying services across California, the Pacific Northwest, and beyond. With offices throughout California and the Portland area, BKF has served transportation, water resources, land development, government, and federal clients for over 110 years.


At BKF, you’ll find a unique opportunity to grow your career with intention. Our culture values professional autonomy, innovation, and meaningful collaboration across offices. We are proud to be recognized with the Great Place to Work certification for the second year in a row.


BKF Engineers is seeking an experienced Qualified SWPPP Developer (QSD) to support construction\-phase environmental compliance services throughout California. The ideal candidate will have approximately 8\+ years of experience managing stormwater compliance programs, conducting construction inspections, implementing Storm Water Pollution Prevention Plans (SWPPPs), coordinating with contractors and agency personnel, and serving as a trusted advisor to project teams. This position will play a key role in helping clients maintain compliance with California's Construction General Permit while supporting BKF's multidisciplinary transportation, municipal, utility, and infrastructure projects.

**Responsibilities**

* Serve as the designated QSD \& QSP for construction projects in accordance with California Construction General Permit requirements
* Conduct routine and rain event inspections, site assessments, and compliance audits
* Implement and oversee SWPPP requirements, BMPs, erosion and sediment control measures, and corrective actions
* Review project sites for permit compliance and identify potential environmental risks
* Coordinate with contractors, construction managers, regulatory agencies, and client representatives to resolve stormwater compliance issues
* Prepare inspection reports, corrective action reports, sampling records, rainfall documentation, and regulatory correspondence
* Perform stormwater monitoring, sampling, data management, and QA/QC review of field documentation
* Assist in the preparation, review, and amendment of SWPPPs and Permit Registration Documents (PRDs)
* Provide technical support related to dewatering activities, spill prevention planning, hazardous materials management, and environmental compliance during construction
* Support proposal development, scope preparation, cost estimating, and project pursuits
* Mentor and train junior inspectors and environmental compliance staff
Collaborate with BKF project managers, engineers, and environmental specialists  
* 

  

  

**Requirements**  

* Bachelor's degree in Environmental Science, Environmental Engineering, Civil Engineering, Geology, or related field
* Current QSP certification required; QSD certification preferred
* Minimum 8 years of experience in stormwater compliance, environmental inspection, or construction environmental services
* Strong knowledge of California Construction General Permit (CGP), SWPPPs, BMP implementation, stormwater monitoring, and regulatory reporting
* Experience supporting transportation, utility, municipal, water, or civil infrastructure projects
* Ability to independently manage multiple projects and field assignments
* Strong technical writing, communication, and organizational skills
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite and electronic stormwater reporting systems
* Valid driver’s license and ability to travel to project sites throughout California


Physical Demands: Must be able to regularly talk, hear and operate a computer, keyboard and mouse and occasionally lift, pull/push and carry up to 20 pounds with accommodations.


Work Conditions: Exposure to constant or intermittent sounds at a low or moderate level consistent with an office setting. Exposure to high\-stress, fast\-paced, deadline\-oriented environment. Travel to other BKF locations and client sites may be required.

 **Benefits**  

* The typical base salary range for this position is $100,000\.00\-$145,000\.00 annualized depending upon skills, experience, education, and geographical location. This is a salary position paid biweekly.
* Competitive salaries, profit sharing, and 401k.
* Generous paid time off packages.
* 9 Paid Holidays.
* Flexible schedules.
* Education reimbursement, Paid annual dues for professional and societal organizations.
* BKF offers competitive and award\-winning benefits and perks. To learn more click here.

BKF provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.


BKF does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters or employment agencies. In the event a recruiter or agency submits a resume or candidate without a previously signed agreement and a specific solicitation for the position or other approved engagement request for the position with BKF, BKF reserves the right to pursue and hire those candidate(s) without any financial obligation to the recruiter or agency. Any unsolicited submittals are done at the risk of the recruiter.


\#LI\-Hybrid

jobs.title: Data Manager (Oil, Gas, Remediation)
jobs.description:
**Company Description** **Work with Us. Change the World.**


At AECOM, we're delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world's trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the world’s most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations.


There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of over 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world.


We're one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us.

 **Job Description**  

AECOM is hiring a Data Manager to oversee maintenance of data infrastructure and architecture for Oil, Gas, and Remediation projects. The successful candidate will drive data generation, storage, and processing initiatives while ensuring the implementation of effective data management solutions and best practices.

**Duties include but are not limited to:**

* Possess an understanding of end\-to\-end remediation project workflows to drive process improvements.
* Maintain databases and data processing systems.
* Ensures data is accessible, reliable, secure, and efficiently processed.
* Combines, optimizes, and manages multiple large\-scale data sources.
* May be responsible for creating automated reporting systems and data solutions.
* Provides software solutions that move data from its source to internal databases, including data warehouses.
* Responsible for maintenance of data pipelines to support data analysis and reporting.
* Analyzes complex issues and recommends solutions that improve data storage processes and methodologies.
* Uses judgment outside defined parameters and precedents to make decisions and resolve business and technical challenges.
* Accountable for the results of assigned projects and processes.
* Demonstrates strong proficiency in data programming languages.
* Demonstrates strong knowledge of data optimization tools, data structures, data architecture, and data warehousing solutions.
* Demonstrates strong knowledge of API (Application Programming Interface) integration to support data access, collection, distribution, transformation, and ELT (Extract, Load, Transform) processes.
* Applies algorithmic and statistical methods, data processing tools, and AI\-enabled approaches to support efficient data consumption and analysis.

  

**Qualifications** **Minimum Requirements:**

* BA/BS Computer Science, Information Science, Data Engineering or related field and 6 years of relevant experience or demonstrated equivalency of experience and/or education.

**Preferred Qualifications:**

* Experience with environmental software implementation in a consulting / O\&G environment, including knowledge in one or more EDM software package(s) such as Earthsoft EQuIS Suite (Professional, Enterprise, EnviroInsite, etc.) and Locus EIM strongly preferred.
* Experience in environmental data management, chemistry, analytical data validation, environmental data analysis and/or visualization.
* Experience in data engineering, business intelligence (BI), or analytics development.
* Strong proficiency in SQL and experience with relational and analytical databases. Proficiency in Python or a similar scripting language.
* Experience developing interactive dashboards and data visualizations using Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar business intelligence tools.
* Experience designing and supporting data pipelines, data integration, and ETL/ELT processes.
* Experience working with cloud\-based or enterprise data warehousing solutions.
* Familiarity with enterprise business systems such as Oracle, Workday, CMiC, or similar enterprise platforms.
* Advanced Microsoft Suite skills, including the Power Platform, with proficiency in data analysis, automation, and reporting.
* Understanding of data quality, data governance, and metadata management. Follow naming convention guidance and provide proper documentation as required.
* Commitment to working in a collaborative, team environment.

  

**Additional Information**  

* This position does not include sponsorship for United States work authorization now or in the future.
* This position does not include relocation


At AECOM, we are committed to maintaining a secure and trustworthy recruitment process and take any fraudulent hiring activity seriously. ***To support this commitment, all newly hired employees are required to attend an in\-person Day 1 onboarding at an AECOM office location as a condition of employment.***

 **About AECOM**


AECOM is proud to offer comprehensive benefits to meet the diverse needs of our employees. Depending on your employment status, AECOM benefits may include medical, dental, vision, life, AD\&D, disability benefits, paid time off, leaves of absences, voluntary benefits, perks, flexible work options, well\-being resources, employee assistance program, business travel insurance, service recognition awards, retirement savings plan, and employee stock purchase plan.


AECOM is the global infrastructure leader, committed to delivering a better world. As a trusted professional services firm powered by deep technical abilities, we solve our clients’ complex challenges in water, environment, energy, transportation and buildings. Our teams partner with public\- and private\-sector clients to create innovative, sustainable and resilient solutions throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm that had revenue of $16\.1 billion in fiscal year 20

This position will play a key role in helping clients maintain compliance with California's Construction General Permit while supporting BKF's multidisciplinary transportation, municipal, utility, and infrastructure projects.

**Responsibilities**

* Serve as the designated QSD \& QSP for construction projects in accordance with California Construction General Permit requirements
* Conduct routine and rain event inspections, site assessments, and compliance audits
* Implement and oversee SWPPP requirements, BMPs, erosion and sediment control measures, and corrective actions
* Review project sites for permit compliance and identify potential environmental risks
* Coordinate with contractors, construction managers, regulatory agencies, and client representatives to resolve stormwater compliance issues
* Prepare inspection reports, corrective action reports, sampling records, rainfall documentation, and regulatory correspondence
* Perform stormwater monitoring, sampling, data management, and QA/QC review of field documentation
* Assist in the preparation, review, and amendment of SWPPPs and Permit Registration Documents (PRDs)
* Provide technical support related to dewatering activities, spill prevention planning, hazardous materials management, and environmental compliance during construction
* Support proposal development, scope preparation, cost estimating, and project pursuits
* Mentor and train junior inspectors and environmental compliance staff
Collaborate with BKF project managers, engineers, and environmental specialists  
* 

  

  

**Requirements**  

* Bachelor's degree in Environmental Science, Environmental Engineering, Civil Engineering, Geology, or related field
* Current QSP certification required; QSD certification preferred
* Minimum 8 years of experience in stormwater compliance, environmental inspection, or construction environmental services
* Strong knowledge of California Construction General Permit (CGP), SWPPPs, BMP implementation, stormwater monitoring, and regulatory reporting
* Experience supporting transportation, utility, municipal, water, or civil infrastructure projects
* Ability to independently manage multiple projects and field assignments
* Strong technical writing, communication, and organizational skills
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite and electronic stormwater reporting systems
* Valid driver’s license and ability to travel to project sites throughout California


Physical Demands: Must be able to regularly talk, hear and operate a computer, keyboard and mouse and occasionally lift, pull/push and carry up to 20 pounds with accommodations.


Work Conditions: Exposure to constant or intermittent sounds at a low or moderate level consistent with an office setting. Exposure to high\-stress, fast\-paced, deadline\-oriented environment. Travel to other BKF locations and client sites may be required.

 **Benefits**  

* The typical base salary range for this position is $100,000\.00\-$145,000\.00 annualized depending upon skills, experience, education, and geographical location. This is a salary position paid biweekly.
* Competitive salaries, profit sharing, and 401k.
* Generous paid time off packages.
* 9 Paid Holidays.
* Flexible schedules.
* Education reimbursement, Paid annual dues for professional and societal organizations.
* BKF offers competitive and award\-winning benefits and perks. To learn more click here.

BKF provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.


BKF does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters or employment agencies. In the event a recruiter or agency submits a resume or candidate without a previously signed agreement and a specific solicitation for the position or other approved engagement request for the position with BKF, BKF reserves the right to pursue and hire those candidate(s) without any financial obligation to the recruiter or agency. Any unsolicited submittals are done at the risk of the recruiter.
